evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 10 Questions but page shows ~22 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Post is 2219 words (pillar) — include a <nav aria-label="Table of contents"> for LLM outline extraction
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 5/10 — Has some voice ('this is the job now') but mostly reads like branded editorial copy with consistent cadence.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Does take stances (Duke's is touristy and fine, skip Hanauma at your peril, WhatsApp is for ranting) so opinions come through.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — A few good turns ('hangover half of the group', 'stare-down from staff') but lots of SEO-flavored stock phrasing.
Quotable sentences 4/10 — A few near-quotables, but most lines are functional itinerary prose rather than memorable.
Intro earns attention 4/10 — Opens with a generic promise and TL;DR/meta clutter before the first real sentence; fails to hook.

specificity 6/10 — Most of the post is strongly anchored in named places, prices, and logistics—but Day 5's lunch entry collapses into vagueness ('Casual spots near Kailua Beach') after the body text correctly names Kalapawai Market. The bullet contradicts the specificity of the paragraph above it and is the most extractable failure point. The rewrite pulls the named location already present in the body copy into the bullet where readers are most likely scanning.
